title: Yao's Conjecture (Quantum Sorting Lower Bound)
statement_text: The quantum query complexity of sorting under partial information, given any poset $P$ on $n$ elements, is at least $c \log(|\Delta(P)|)$ for some universal constant $c > 0$.
context_text: Yao (STOC'04) proved a weaker lower bound of $c\log(|\Delta(P)|) - c'n$ for universal constants $c, c' > 0$, which is useless when the information-theoretic lower bound is $o(n)$. He conjectured that the linear correction term is unnecessary and the information-theoretic lower bound holds for any partial order $P$ up to a constant factor. Section 4 of the paper is titled 'A proof of Yao's conjecture for series-parallel posets'.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the class of series-parallel posets (Theorem 2) and extends it to the wider class of extended series-parallel posets, which includes posets with $\Omega(n^4)$ induced $N$-subposets (Corollary 1).
paper_title: Information-theoretic lower bounds for quantum sorting

title: Conjecture on LB and QLB equivalence
statement_text: The two lower bounds $\mathrm{LB}(P)$ and $\mathrm{QLB}(P)$ are within a constant factor of each other for all posets $P$.
context_text: In the Conclusion, the authors present a table comparing the classical quantity $\mathrm{LB}(P) = n(\ln n - H(P))$ and the quantum quantity $\mathrm{QLB}(P) = n(H_n - QH(P))$, where $H(P) = \min_{z \in C(P)} h(z)$ is the entropy and $QH(P) = \mathbb{E}_{z \in C(P)}[h(z)]$ is its averaged variant. The authors state their findings support a conjecture relating these two bounds, but the remainder of the statement is corrupted in the PDF source.
notes: PDF source — the statement is truncated after 'QLB(P)' and replaced with garbled content (a list of arXiv IDs). The statement above is inferred from context; the exact formulation cannot be verified.
paper_title: Information-theoretic lower bounds for quantum sorting
